title: Spec Staff rebellion caused radical changes to NME Split
display_title: Spec Staff rebellion caused radical changes to NME Split
wikitext:
[]: Spec: Staff rebellion caused radical changes to NME? [Split] Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 28, 2013 6:12 pm [Split from right after this post. - Monk] It's worth noting that towards the end of the 2.0 production, the staff were almost in open rebellion demanding that the script introduce new material. I think that the time skip had more to do with Khara being bored recreating the original series.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jun 28, 2013 6:17 pm I don't recall that being reported before. Seems strange, then, that almost all the new material that had been introduced in Ha was so unceremoniously dropped on the floor.

[]: Post by Stillborn » Fri Jun 28, 2013 6:32 pm Sooo... Maybe original 3.0 was supposed to happen right after ending of 2.0, and events of Q supposed to be in fourth movie that would end in a downer ending of three kids on the toxic desert. But becuase Khara was bored, they pushed Q to be slightly changed and become the third movie to get to the new stuff faster and FINAL will have to top Q in downer ending now

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jun 28, 2013 6:33 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It's worth noting that towards the end of the 2.0 production, the staff were almost in open rebellion demanding that the script introduce new material. I think that the time skip had more to do with Khara being bored recreating the original series.

[]: Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Jun 28, 2013 8:33 pm This'll assuredly be something answered in the 3.0 CRC interviews, whenever they come out.

[]: Re: Spec: Staff rebellion caused radical changes to NME? [Sp Post by TheFriskyIan » Fri Jun 28, 2013 10:01 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It's worth noting that towards the end of the 2.0 production, the staff were almost in open rebellion demanding that the script introduce new material. I think that the time skip had more to do with Khara being bored recreating the original series.

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jun 28, 2013 10:30 pm Is there any proof about this supposed staff rebellion?

[]: Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Jun 29, 2013 1:01 am I don't think the introduction of Mari, Asuka's Bardiel encounter, and the whole thing with Zeruel and Eva-01's pseudo-evolution can be considered anywhere near reproducing the same script. In fact very little of 2.0 actually matches the original script for the episodes it covers. Admittedly 1.0 was pretty much a carbon copy of Episodes 1-6, but that was only the first film. I don't see how the different-ness of 2.0 was so insubstantial as to cause staff to riot over it.

[]: Post by Jurrasic » Sat Jun 29, 2013 1:11 pm Yeah, this is an interesting tidbit that can go far to explaining why 3.0 went in such a batshit insane direction after 2.0, and especially why what we got so completely disregards anything at all that was shown in the end of 2.0 preview for 3.0. Mathic man, do you have any link or proof of this near-mutiny? It would surely make me feel a whole lot better about 3.0 if so!

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jun 29, 2013 4:38 pm Thinking on it further, it would be more reasonable to believe any such event happening during Jo, which was much closer to a direct remake with better graphics, at least until the end of Operation Yashima, than at the end of Ha.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 29, 2013 5:19 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Thinking on it further, it would be more reasonable to believe any such event happening during Jo, which was much closer to a direct remake with better graphics, at least until the end of Operation Yashima, than at the end of Ha.

[]: Post by iihieiii » Sat Jun 29, 2013 6:06 pm Well....if is this true...and considering that the production time of 3.0 was almost doubled from 1.0-2.0 time....it's possible...better yet....we should see 4 soon then ;P

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jun 29, 2013 8:20 pm My already failing memory managed to exaggerate this interview into some kind of staff rebellion. The relevant passage was the following Anno Interview wrote: --- What was the impetus for the change? Anno: The big thing was the comments made by Makki (Director Kazuya Tsurumaki's pet name). "For this, we can't make films just by summarizing the TV series." He also said, "I want Asuka to have a 'film-like' role. At this rate, she will just be making an appearance - she provides no drama vis-a-vis Shinji, the protagonist; she is not involved." Furthermore, "As a story, it's not different enough from the original series" - all these in succession. He further said, "Isn't a change as drastic as having Asuka pilot Unit-03 necessary?" I think, because this was a major, fundamental change, opinion was divided when I researched the views of a number of staff members. (Ikki) Todoroki, in particular, was tremendously opposed. After thinking about it, I ultimately adopted Tsurumaki's proposal and decided to change the pilot of Unit-03. That was the sixth draft of the script. It was finished following the 2007 new year, on January 8th. In this draft, the test pilot was simply changed from Touji to Asuka, [and the reason for it - Mari, the new pilot, is reassigned to NERV HQ from Europe, and Asuka will be taken off of Unit-02, so she volunteers - was really just improvised. ?] The change of test pilot was forced into the draft, with the characters and situations left unaltered. .... In addition, one more new, significant change emerged at this time. Abandoning the scenario where Rei self-destructs in the last scene to save Shinji, I decided on a course where Shinji saves Rei. This was due to (Toshimichi) Otsuki's opinion as a producer that, because [the story of the second part would also lead to the third part ?], he wanted it to end hopefully, with a positive feeling. Regarding this, opinions among the staff were again divided. This time, it was Tsurumaki who particularily opposed, or resisted, this change. Makki said that he wanted to prominently feature Rei III, who was barely present in the old work, in the third part [of Rebuild], developing her in depth. It seemed like this had been one of his motivations for participating [in Rebuild]. However, in the end I decided on the scenario where Rei is saved, and he assented, saying "If it's all right with Anno-san....". I think I decided that way because, with "Break," I was searching for something called "change." As well, I thought this would lead to the next change. At this point I could generally see what the roles of Asuka, Rei, and Misato would be. The problem was Mari.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 29, 2013 8:28 pm ^ Anno said that this discussion happened in well before production started on 1.0, and Anno says that the finished drafts reflecting those changed were finished January 8, 2007. Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone was released to Japanese theaters September 1, 2007. Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance was released to Japanese theaters June 27, 2009. This wasn't a "rebellion" made by the staff post 2.0 production inasmuch as it was something the creative staff had to discuss, agree upon, and adhere to so everyone could get on the same page and pitch in their creative 2 cents as to the best path for these 4 whole expensive and time consuming movies to take prior to actually making them. So, there WAS no staff rebellion post 2.0. By that time everyone had known for a few years now where these movies were going, and kept it secret from us viewers until the 3.0 film was released. The questions have been answered. This thread can be retired?

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jun 29, 2013 9:24 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: This thread can be retired?
